strategy_summary: The binary is a 64-bit ELF with NX enabled and no PIE. The vulnerable function reads up to 0x200 bytes into a 128-byte buffer, causing a stack overflow. We can use ROP to call system('/bin/sh') to get a shell. We need to find the offset to control the return address, and we already have the pop_rdi_ret gadget. We will also need to leak a libc address to calculate the base address of libc and then call system('/bin/sh').
